# Timothy Davison

> Timothy Davison (Tim) is CEO and Cofounder of Ako Biotica Inc., the maker of CellWalk, an immersive biology education app for Apple Vision Pro, iPad, and iPhone. CellWalk lets users explore a detailed 3D bacterial cell from the whole-cell scale down to atoms, and uses Apple's on-device Foundation Models framework to generate adaptive, grounded conversational explanations for each learner. It was named a 2025 Apple Design Awards Finalist (Visuals and Graphics) and featured by the Apple Newsroom. Tim has a PhD in computer graphics from the University of Calgary, with a background in realtime simulation and AI. Ako Biotica's mission is to inspire the next generation of scientists.

## About

- [Homepage](https://timd.ca/)
- Role: CEO and Cofounder, Ako Biotica Inc.
- Background: PhD, Computer Science (University of Calgary); graphics programmer; realtime simulation and agent-based modelling.
- Mission: inspire the next generation of scientists.

## CellWalk

CellWalk is an immersive biology education app available on Apple Vision Pro, iPad, and iPhone. Users take guided molecular tours of a whole bacterial cell or explore on their own, zooming continuously from the whole-cell scale down to atoms. CellWalk was created by Timothy Davison and HK. The molecular model and guided tour are based on the work of David Goodsell, Ludovic Autin, and Martina Maritan, drawing on Maritan et al.'s 2022 *Journal of Molecular Biology* paper "Building Structural Models of a Whole Mycoplasma Cell" with co-authors Jonathan Karr, Markus W. Covert, and Arthur J. Olson.

- [CellWalk website](https://cellwalk.ca/)
- [CellWalk on the App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/cellwalk/id1627776848)
- [Maritan et al. (2022)](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jmb.2021.167351)

## Recognition and press

- [Apple Newsroom — Foundation Models framework feature (Sept 2025)](https://www.apple.com/ca/newsroom/2025/09/apples-foundation-models-framework-unlocks-new-intelligent-app-experiences/): CellWalk is profiled as an education app that uses Apple's on-device Foundation Models framework to generate adaptive, tool-grounded conversational explanations of molecular biology, with user-profile-aware learning.
- Apple Design Awards 2025 Finalist — Visuals and Graphics ([newsroom announcement](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2025/06/apple-unveils-winners-and-finalists-of-the-2025-apple-design-awards/), June 3, 2025).
- [UCalgary alumni story (June 17, 2025)](https://www.ucalgary.ca/news/ucalgary-alums-app-earns-recognition-apple) — University of Calgary covers the ADA finalist nod.
- [What the Tech? Podcast — University of Calgary (March 2026)](https://open.spotify.com/episode/3zAul4gZfHDNcI1qmKbjzx): conversation about Tim's path into computer science and biology, spatial computing, real-time simulation, and the story behind CellWalk.

## Talks

- *Thoughtful Spatial Design: Exploring Biology from Cells to Atoms* — [KWDC25, Seoul, September 2025](https://kwdc.dev/). On the design principles behind CellWalk for Apple Vision Pro.
- Panelist, *The Interface at WWDC25* (June 2025) — a fireside chat hosted by UX designer Tamia James with Apple Design Awards 2025 winners and finalists.

## Research

Tim's PhD thesis, *LifeBrush*, is about painting 3D molecular landscapes in interactive virtual reality and bringing them to life with realtime coarse-grained agent-based simulation. The thesis received an honorable mention at the SIGGRAPH 2020 Thesis Fast Forward.

- [LifeBrush PhD thesis (PRISM, University of Calgary)](https://prism.ucalgary.ca/handle/1880/111493)
- [LifeBrush: painting, simulating, and visualizing dense biomolecular environments — Computers & Graphics, 2019](https://timd.ca/publication/lifebrush_cg/)
- [Interactive example-palettes for discrete element texture synthesis — Computers & Graphics, 2018](https://timd.ca/publication/discreteelements_cg/)
- [LifeBrush: Painting Interactive Agent-Based Simulations — Cyberworlds, 2018](https://timd.ca/publication/lifebrush_cw/)
